theorem: or_comm (P Q : Prop) (h : P ∨ Q) : Q ∨ P

steps.label: Case split on the disjunction
steps.explanation: h : P ∨ Q could be either side. We split into two cases: one where P is true, one where Q is true. This creates two subgoals.

steps.label: Case 1: we have P, prove Q ∨ P via the right
steps.explanation: We have hp : P. To prove Q ∨ P, we use Or.inr to inject into the right side of the disjunction.

steps.label: Case 2: we have Q, prove Q ∨ P via the left
steps.explanation: Now in the second case, we have hq : Q. To prove Q ∨ P, we use Or.inl to inject into the left side.
